title: Sleight of Hand: The Tale of Mara Jade
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb}} {{ShortStory |image= |title="Sleight of Hand:
The Tale of Mara Jade" |author=[[Timothy Zahn]] |illustrator= |editor=[[Kevin J. Anderson]] |publisher=[[Bantam Spectra]] |release date=[[December]] [[1995]]{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/3549/tales-from-jabbas-palace-star-wars-legends-by-kevin-anderson-editor|text=Tales from Jabba's Palace: Star Wars Legends}} |published in=''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]'' |reprinted in= |pages=19 |timeline=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} "'''Sleight of Hand: The Tale of Mara Jade'''" is a short story written by [[Timothy Zahn]] for ''[[Tales from Jabba's Palace]]''. It features [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] as a dancer in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]]. ==Plot summary== [[File:Arica.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Mara as "Arica" in Jabba's Palace]] Following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] sends [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] to infiltrate [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], disguised as the [[Huttese slave-girl costume|dancing girl]] Arica, in order to [[Death/Legends|assassinate]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Her dark [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] believes that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] may be courting Skywalker as an apprentice to unseat him. However, [[Melina Carniss]], another of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] dancers and a member of Jabba's secret security detail, suspects her of planning to assassinate the Hutt, and attempts to [[Prison/Legends|arrest]] her. Jade turns the tables on Carniss by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to cause Melina to shoot one of Jabba's [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards. Jade flees down a tunnel and comes to a ventilation shaft of the [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] pit. After fighting through three rows of guards, she is able to watch Luke kill [[Jabba's rancor/Legends|Jabba's rancor]]. She then enters the ventilation shaft of the rancor pit using a [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]], and uses the Force to open the trapdoor and follow the crowd out to the [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]]. Though she begs Jabba to let her join him and others at the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]], a suspicious Jabba instead provides her with a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and instructs her to leave and never come back after she tried to use the Force on him. Thus stymied in this attempt to fulfill her mission, Jade returns to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]] and to a displeased Palpatine. ==Continuity== Arranging [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade's]] appearances in chronological order means readers would witness her failure to [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] first, prior to Luke actually meeting her in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' ([[1991]]), her first published appearance. As such, this reading order dispelled the brief mystery surrounding her animosity toward Luke as presented in ''Heir''. Mara adopted the alias Celina Marniss in "[[First Contact]]" ([[1994]]), a short story published first but set much later in the timeline. Mara exhibited very articulate [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] communication with the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]], a use of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] telepathy far more precise than shown elsewhere in the [[Star Wars Legends|canon]]. Such a display of power needed to be vetted by [[George Lucas]], as a July 19, [[1994]], memo indicates. Lucas specified that this telepathy is due to an innate difference in Mara Jade herself and should be limited to her alone. A popular misconception around the time of this short story's publication was that Mara Jade could be spotted somewhere in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] ([[1983]]). Given that her character was conceived years after that film was produced and that no unnamed extras have been retroactively identified as "Arica," Mara was not actually in the movie. ==Media==
